Set on <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">passkey_config</span><span class="fn-python">passkey_config</span><span class="fn-swift">passkeyConfig</span><span class="fn-kotlin">passkeyConfig</span><span class="fn-javascript">passkeyConfig</span><span class="fn-react-native">passkeyConfig</span><span class="fn-flutter">passkeyConfig</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyConfig</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyConfig</span></code>. |

Configure the built-in provider through <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">provider_options</span><span class="fn-python">provider_options</span><span class="fn-swift">providerOptions</span><span class="fn-kotlin">providerOptions</span><span class="fn-javascript">providerOptions</span><span class="fn-react-native">providerOptions</span><span class="fn-flutter">providerOptions</span><span class="fn-go">ProviderOptions</span><span class="fn-csharp">ProviderOptions</span></code> on <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">passkey_config</span><span class="fn-python">passkey_config</span><span class="fn-swift">passkeyConfig</span><span class="fn-kotlin">passkeyConfig</span><span class="fn-javascript">passkeyConfig</span><span class="fn-react-native">passkeyConfig</span><span class="fn-flutter">passkeyConfig</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyConfig</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyConfig</span></code> (a <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PasskeyProviderOptions</span><span class="fn-python">PasskeyProviderOptions</span><span class="fn-swift">PasskeyProviderOptions</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PasskeyProviderOptions</span><span class="fn-javascript">PasskeyProviderOptions</span><span class="fn-react-native">PasskeyProviderOptions</span><span class="fn-flutter">PasskeyProviderOptions</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyProviderOptions</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyProviderOptions</span></code>):

| Field | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">rp_id</span><span class="fn-python">rp_id</span><span class="fn-swift">rpId</span><span class="fn-kotlin">rpId</span><span class="fn-javascript">rpId</span><span class="fn-react-native">rpId</span><span class="fn-flutter">rpId</span><span class="fn-go">RpId</span><span class="fn-csharp">RpId</span></code> | Breez shared RP | Relying Party ID: your app's domain, or unset for the Breez shared RP (`keys.breez.technology`) if your app is Breez-registered. Changing it later strands existing credentials. |
| <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">rp_name</span><span class="fn-python">rp_name</span><span class="fn-swift">rpName</span><span class="fn-kotlin">rpName</span><span class="fn-javascript">rpName</span><span class="fn-react-native">rpName</span><span class="fn-flutter">rpName</span><span class="fn-go">RpName</span><span class="fn-csharp">RpName</span></code> | `"Breez"` | Display name for your app, shown in some authenticator UIs. |
| <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">user_name</span><span class="fn-python">user_name</span><span class="fn-swift">userName</span><span class="fn-kotlin">userName</span><span class="fn-javascript">userName</span><span class="fn-react-native">userName</span><span class="fn-flutter">userName</span><span class="fn-go">UserName</span><span class="fn-csharp">UserName</span></code> | <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">rp_name</span><span class="fn-python">rp_name</span><span class="fn-swift">rpName</span><span class="fn-kotlin">rpName</span><span class="fn-javascript">rpName</span><span class="fn-react-native">rpName</span><span class="fn-flutter">rpName</span><span class="fn-go">RpName</span><span class="fn-csharp">RpName</span></code> | Account identifier the OS sign-in picker shows beneath the display name, e.g. `john@doe.com`. Set a stable per-user value to keep each registration a distinct entry. |
| <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">user_display_name</span><span class="fn-python">user_display_name</span><span class="fn-swift">userDisplayName</span><span class="fn-kotlin">userDisplayName</span><span class="fn-javascript">userDisplayName</span><span class="fn-react-native">userDisplayName</span><span class="fn-flutter">userDisplayName</span><span class="fn-go">UserDisplayName</span><span class="fn-csharp">UserDisplayName</span></code> | <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">user_name</span><span class="fn-python">user_name</span><span class="fn-swift">userName</span><span class="fn-kotlin">userName</span><span class="fn-javascript">userName</span><span class="fn-react-native">userName</span><span class="fn-flutter">userName</span><span class="fn-go">UserName</span><span class="fn-csharp">UserName</span></code> | Human-friendly name the picker shows most prominently, e.g. `John Doe`. |

For platform-specific provider options (iOS `URLSession` / presentation anchor, Android `Activity`, web `authenticatorAttachment`) or a custom PRF backend, build the provider yourself and inject it. See [PRF providers](./passkey_prf_providers.md).

### Checking passkey availability

Call <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PasskeyClient.check_availability</span><span class="fn-python">PasskeyClient.check_availability</span><span class="fn-swift">PasskeyClient.checkAvailability</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PasskeyClient.checkAvailability</span><span class="fn-javascript">PasskeyClient.checkAvailability</span><span class="fn-react-native">PasskeyClient.checkAvailability</span><span class="fn-flutter">PasskeyClient.checkAvailability</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyClient.CheckAvailability</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyClient.CheckAvailability</span></code> before showing the passkey button. One call covers device support and your domain config, so you can hide the option on unsupported devices (older Android / iOS) or surface a configuration error (missing entitlement, undeployed AASA) before the user runs into an opaque WebAuthn failure.

<custom-tabs category="lang">
<div slot="title">Rust</div>
<section>

```rust,ignore
match passkey.check_availability().await? {
    PasskeyAvailability::Available => {
        // Show passkey as primary option.
    }
    PasskeyAvailability::PrfUnsupported => {
        // Fall back to mnemonic flow.
    }
    PasskeyAvailability::NotAssociated { source, reason } => {
        eprintln!("Domain association failed (source={source}): {reason}");
    }
    PasskeyAvailability::Skipped { reason: _ } => {
        // No verification source on this platform; proceed normally.
    }
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Swift</div>
<section>

```swift,ignore
switch try await passkey.checkAvailability() {
case .available:
    // Show passkey as primary option.
    break
case .prfUnsupported:
    // Fall back to mnemonic flow.
    break
case .notAssociated(let source, let reason):
    print("Domain association failed (source=\(source)): \(reason)")
case .skipped:
    // No verification source on this platform; proceed normally.
    break
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Kotlin</div>
<section>

```kotlin,ignore
when (val availability = passkey.checkAvailability()) {
    is PasskeyAvailability.Available -> Unit
    is PasskeyAvailability.PrfUnsupported -> Unit
    is PasskeyAvailability.NotAssociated -> {
        // Log.e("Breez", "Domain association failed
        // (source=${availability.source}): ${availability.reason}")
    }
    is PasskeyAvailability.Skipped -> Unit
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">C#</div>
<section>

```csharp,ignore
switch (await passkey.CheckAvailability())
{
    case PasskeyAvailability.Available:
        break;
    case PasskeyAvailability.PrfUnsupported:
        break;
    case PasskeyAvailability.NotAssociated notAssociated:
        Console.WriteLine($"Domain association failed (source={notAssociated.source}): " +
                          $"{notAssociated.reason}");
        break;
    case PasskeyAvailability.Skipped:
        break;
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Javascript</div>
<section>

```typescript,ignore
const availability = await passkey.checkAvailability()
switch (availability.type) {
  case 'available':
    // Show passkey as primary option.
    break
  case 'prfUnsupported':
    // Fall back to mnemonic flow.
    break
  case 'notAssociated':
    console.error(
      `Domain association failed (source=${availability.source}): ${availability.reason}`
    )
    break
  case 'skipped':
    // No verification source on this platform; proceed normally.
    break
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">React Native</div>
<section>

```typescript,ignore
const availability = await passkey.checkAvailability()
switch (availability.tag) {
  case PasskeyAvailability_Tags.Available:
    // Show passkey as primary option.
    break
  case PasskeyAvailability_Tags.PrfUnsupported:
    // Fall back to mnemonic flow.
    break
  case PasskeyAvailability_Tags.NotAssociated:
    console.error(
      `Domain association failed (source=${availability.inner.source}): ` +
      `${availability.inner.reason}`
    )
    break
  case PasskeyAvailability_Tags.Skipped:
    // No verification source on this platform; proceed normally.
    break
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Flutter</div>
<section>

```dart,ignore
final availability = await passkey.checkAvailability();
if (availability is PasskeyAvailability_Available) {
  // Show passkey as primary option.
} else if (availability is PasskeyAvailability_PrfUnsupported) {
  // Fall back to mnemonic flow.
} else if (availability is PasskeyAvailability_NotAssociated) {
  print("Domain association failed (source=${availability.source}): ${availability.reason}");
} else if (availability is PasskeyAvailability_Skipped) {
  // No verification source on this platform; proceed normally.
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Python</div>
<section>

```python,ignore
availability = await passkey.check_availability()
if isinstance(availability, PasskeyAvailability.AVAILABLE):
    # Show passkey as primary option.
    pass
elif isinstance(availability, PasskeyAvailability.PRF_UNSUPPORTED):
    # Fall back to mnemonic flow.
    pass
elif isinstance(availability, PasskeyAvailability.NOT_ASSOCIATED):
    print(f"Domain association failed (source={availability.source}): {availability.reason}")
elif isinstance(availability, PasskeyAvailability.SKIPPED):
    # No verification source on this platform; proceed normally.
    pass
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Go</div>
<section>

```go,ignore
availability, err := passkey.CheckAvailability()
if err != nil {
	return
}
switch r := availability.(type) {
case breez_sdk_spark.PasskeyAvailabilityAvailable:
	// Show passkey as primary option.
	_ = r
case breez_sdk_spark.PasskeyAvailabilityPrfUnsupported:
	// Fall back to mnemonic flow.
	_ = r
case breez_sdk_spark.PasskeyAvailabilityNotAssociated:
	log.Printf("Domain association failed (source=%s): %s", r.Source, r.Reason)
case breez_sdk_spark.PasskeyAvailabilitySkipped:
	// No verification source on this platform; proceed normally.
	_ = r
}
```

</section>

</custom-tabs>


## Choosing a flow

The right flow depends on the platform:

- **iOS / Android** use a single-call unified flow backed by <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PasskeyClient.connect_with_passkey</span><span class="fn-python">PasskeyClient.connect_with_passkey</span><span class="fn-swift">PasskeyClient.connectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PasskeyClient.connectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-javascript">PasskeyClient.connectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-react-native">PasskeyClient.connectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-flutter">PasskeyClient.connectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyClient.ConnectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyClient.ConnectWithPasskey</span></code>.
- **Web** uses two buttons ("Create a new passkey" and "Sign in with a passkey") and lets the user pick.

For explicit control over each path, call <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PasskeyClient.sign_in</span><span class="fn-python">PasskeyClient.sign_in</span><span class="fn-swift">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-javascript">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-react-native">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-flutter">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyClient.SignIn</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyClient.SignIn</span></code> and <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-python">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-swift">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-javascript">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-react-native">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-flutter">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyClient.Register</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyClient.Register</span></code> directly.

### Unified flow (iOS / Android)

One "Use Passkey" button: a silent sign-in for returning users, with automatic fall-through to registration on a fresh device.

The response's <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">credential</span><span class="fn-python">credential</span><span class="fn-swift">credential</span><span class="fn-kotlin">credential</span><span class="fn-javascript">credential</span><span class="fn-react-native">credential</span><span class="fn-flutter">credential</span><span class="fn-go">Credential</span><span class="fn-csharp">Credential</span></code> field carries whichever credential signed in or was registered. See [Credential metadata](./passkey_credential_metadata.md) for using it.

<custom-tabs category="lang">
<div slot="title">Rust</div>
<section>

```rust,ignore
// Single-CTA onboarding: silent sign-in, fall through to register.
let response = passkey
    .connect_with_passkey(ConnectWithPasskeyRequest {
        label: Some("personal".to_string()),
        ..Default::default()
    })
    .await?;

let config = default_config(Network::Mainnet);
let sdk = connect(ConnectRequest {
    config,
    seed: response.wallet.seed,
    storage_dir: "./.data".to_string(),
})
.await?;
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Swift</div>
<section>

```swift,ignore
// Single-CTA onboarding: silent sign-in, fall through to register.
var config = defaultConfig(network: .mainnet)
config.apiKey = "<breez api key>"

let response = try await passkey.connectWithPasskey(
    request: ConnectWithPasskeyRequest(label: "personal")
)

let sdk = try await connect(
    request: ConnectRequest(
        config: config,
        seed: response.wallet.seed,
        storageDir: "./.data"
    ))
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Kotlin</div>
<section>

```kotlin,ignore
// Single-CTA onboarding: silent sign-in, fall through to register.
val config = defaultConfig(Network.MAINNET).apply { apiKey = "<breez api key>" }
val response = passkey.connectWithPasskey(
    ConnectWithPasskeyRequest(label = "personal")
)

val sdk = connect(ConnectRequest(config, response.wallet.seed, "./.data"))
```

</section>

<div slot="title">C#</div>
<section>

```csharp,ignore
// Single-CTA onboarding: silent sign-in for a returning user,
// fall-through to register on a fresh device.
var response = await passkey.ConnectWithPasskey(
    new ConnectWithPasskeyRequest(label: "personal")
);

var config = BreezSdkSparkMethods.DefaultConfig(network: Network.Mainnet);
var sdk = await BreezSdkSparkMethods.Connect(new ConnectRequest(
    config: config,
    seed: response.wallet.seed,
    storageDir: "./.data"
));
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Javascript</div>
<section>

```typescript,ignore
// Not available on web; use two buttons (signIn / register) instead.
const response = await passkey.signIn({ label: 'personal' })

const config = defaultConfig('mainnet')
const sdk = await connect({ config, seed: response.wallet.seed, storageDir: './.data' })
```

</section>

<div slot="title">React Native</div>
<section>

```typescript,ignore
// Silent sign-in, fall through to register.
const config = { ...defaultConfig(Network.Mainnet), apiKey: '<breez api key>' }
const response = await passkey.connectWithPasskey({
  label: 'personal',
  allowCredentials: undefined,
  excludeCredentials: undefined
})

const sdk = await connect({ config, seed: response.wallet.seed, storageDir: './.data' })
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Flutter</div>
<section>

```dart,ignore
// Single-CTA onboarding: silent sign-in, fall through to register.
final config = defaultConfig(network: Network.mainnet)
    .copyWith(apiKey: '<breez api key>');
final response = await passkey.connectWithPasskey(
  request: ConnectWithPasskeyRequest(label: 'personal'),
);

final sdk = await connect(
    request: ConnectRequest(
        config: config, seed: response.wallet.seed, storageDir: "./.data"));
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Python</div>
<section>

```python,ignore
# Silent sign-in for a returning user, fall-through to register on a fresh device.
response = await passkey.connect_with_passkey(
    ConnectWithPasskeyRequest(label="personal")
)

config = default_config(network=Network.MAINNET)
sdk = await connect(
    ConnectRequest(config=config, seed=response.wallet.seed, storage_dir="./.data")
)
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Go</div>
<section>

```go,ignore
// Silent sign-in for a returning user, fall-through to register on a fresh device.
label := "personal"
response, err := passkey.ConnectWithPasskey(breez_sdk_spark.ConnectWithPasskeyRequest{
	Label: &label,
})
if err != nil {
	return nil, err
}

config := breez_sdk_spark.DefaultConfig(breez_sdk_spark.NetworkMainnet)
sdk, err := breez_sdk_spark.Connect(breez_sdk_spark.ConnectRequest{
	Config:     config,
	Seed:       response.Wallet.Seed,
	StorageDir: "./.data",
})
if err != nil {
	return nil, err
}
```

</section>

</custom-tabs>


### Two-button flow (Web)

On web, present two buttons: **Create a new passkey** (calls <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-python">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-swift">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-javascript">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-react-native">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-flutter">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyClient.Register</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyClient.Register</span></code>) and **Sign in with a passkey** (calls <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PasskeyClient.sign_in</span><span class="fn-python">PasskeyClient.sign_in</span><span class="fn-swift">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-javascript">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-react-native">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-flutter">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyClient.SignIn</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyClient.SignIn</span></code>).

<code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PasskeyClient.connect_with_passkey</span><span class="fn-python">PasskeyClient.connect_with_passkey</span><span class="fn-swift">PasskeyClient.connectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PasskeyClient.connectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-javascript">PasskeyClient.connectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-react-native">PasskeyClient.connectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-flutter">PasskeyClient.connectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyClient.ConnectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyClient.ConnectWithPasskey</span></code> is not available on the WASM target. WebAuthn reports "no credential" and "user cancelled" identically, so the SDK can't auto-detect the flow. Let the user choose.

### Sign in and register

Call <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PasskeyClient.sign_in</span><span class="fn-python">PasskeyClient.sign_in</span><span class="fn-swift">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-javascript">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-react-native">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-flutter">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyClient.SignIn</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyClient.SignIn</span></code> and <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-python">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-swift">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-javascript">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-react-native">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-flutter">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyClient.Register</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyClient.Register</span></code> directly for explicit control: the two web buttons, separate create-a-passkey and sign-in screens, or adding a new label for a returning user. Pass `wallet.seed` to <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">connect</span><span class="fn-python">connect</span><span class="fn-swift">connect</span><span class="fn-kotlin">connect</span><span class="fn-javascript">connect</span><span class="fn-react-native">connect</span><span class="fn-flutter">connect</span><span class="fn-go">Connect</span><span class="fn-csharp">Connect</span></code> in either case.

#### Sign in

Sign in to an existing credential:

<custom-tabs category="lang">
<div slot="title">Rust</div>
<section>

```rust,ignore
// Returning-user-only sign-in. No fall-through to register.
Ok(passkey
    .sign_in(SignInRequest {
        label: Some("personal".to_string()),
        ..Default::default()
    })
    .await?)
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Swift</div>
<section>

```swift,ignore
// Returning-user sign-in. No fall-through to register.
return try await passkey.signIn(request: SignInRequest(label: "personal"))
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Kotlin</div>
<section>

```kotlin,ignore
// Returning-user sign-in. No fall-through to register.
return passkey.signIn(SignInRequest(label = "personal"))
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Javascript</div>
<section>

```typescript,ignore
// Returning-user sign-in. No fall-through to register.
return await passkey.signIn({ label: 'personal' })
```

</section>

<div slot="title">React Native</div>
<section>

```typescript,ignore
// Returning-user sign-in. No fall-through to register.
return await passkey.signIn({
  label: 'personal',
  allowCredentials: undefined,
  preferImmediatelyAvailableCredentials: undefined
})
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Flutter</div>
<section>

```dart,ignore
// Returning-user sign-in. No fall-through to register.
return await passkey.signIn(request: SignInRequest(label: 'personal'));
```

</section>

</custom-tabs>


#### Register

Register a fresh credential:

<custom-tabs category="lang">
<div slot="title">Rust</div>
<section>

```rust,ignore
let response = passkey
    .register(RegisterRequest {
        label: Some("personal".to_string()),
        ..Default::default()
    })
    .await?;

let config = default_config(Network::Mainnet);
let sdk = connect(ConnectRequest {
    config,
    seed: response.wallet.seed,
    storage_dir: "./.data".to_string(),
})
.await?;
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Swift</div>
<section>

```swift,ignore
var config = defaultConfig(network: .mainnet)
config.apiKey = "<breez api key>"

let response = try await passkey.register(
    request: RegisterRequest(label: "personal")
)

let sdk = try await connect(
    request: ConnectRequest(
        config: config,
        seed: response.wallet.seed,
        storageDir: "./.data"
    ))
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Kotlin</div>
<section>

```kotlin,ignore
val config = defaultConfig(Network.MAINNET).apply { apiKey = "<breez api key>" }
val response = passkey.register(RegisterRequest(label = "personal"))

val sdk = connect(ConnectRequest(config, response.wallet.seed, "./.data"))
```

</section>

```csharp,ignore
    config: config,

    storageDir: "./.data"
));
```

<div slot="title">Javascript</div>

```typescript,ignore
const config = { ...defaultConfig(Network.Mainnet), apiKey: '<breez api key>' }
```

```dart,ignore
  request: RegisterRequest(label: 'personal'),
```

```go,ignore
	Seed:       response.Wallet.Seed,
```

## Error recovery

Every passkey failure normalizes to a <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PrfProviderError</span><span class="fn-python">PrfProviderError</span><span class="fn-swift">PrfProviderError</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PrfProviderError</span><span class="fn-javascript">PrfProviderError</span><span class="fn-react-native">PrfProviderError</span><span class="fn-flutter">PrfProviderError</span><span class="fn-go">PrfProviderError</span><span class="fn-csharp">PrfProviderError</span></code> variant. Match on the variant to drive recovery:

| Variant | What it means | Recommended action |
|---|---|---|
| <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PrfProviderError::UserCancelled</span><span class="fn-python">PrfProviderError.USER_CANCELLED</span><span class="fn-swift">PrfProviderError.userCancelled</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PrfProviderError.UserCancelled</span><span class="fn-javascript">PrfProviderError.UserCancelled</span><span class="fn-react-native">PrfProviderError.UserCancelled</span><span class="fn-flutter">PrfProviderError.UserCancelled</span><span class="fn-go">PrfProviderErrorUserCancelled</span><span class="fn-csharp">PrfProviderError.UserCancelled</span></code> | User dismissed the OS prompt | Sticky retry UI with "Try Again". |
| <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PrfProviderError::CredentialNotFound</span><span class="fn-python">PrfProviderError.CREDENTIAL_NOT_FOUND</span><span class="fn-swift">PrfProviderError.credentialNotFound</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PrfProviderError.CredentialNotFound</span><span class="fn-javascript">PrfProviderError.CredentialNotFound</span><span class="fn-react-native">PrfProviderError.CredentialNotFound</span><span class="fn-flutter">PrfProviderError.CredentialNotFound</span><span class="fn-go">PrfProviderErrorCredentialNotFound</span><span class="fn-csharp">PrfProviderError.CredentialNotFound</span></code> | No matching credential on this device | Fall through to <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-python">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-swift">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-javascript">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-react-native">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-flutter">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyClient.Register</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyClient.Register</span></code>. |
| <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PrfProviderError::CredentialAlreadyExists</span><span class="fn-python">PrfProviderError.CREDENTIAL_ALREADY_EXISTS</span><span class="fn-swift">PrfProviderError.credentialAlreadyExists</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PrfProviderError.CredentialAlreadyExists</span><span class="fn-javascript">PrfProviderError.CredentialAlreadyExists</span><span class="fn-react-native">PrfProviderError.CredentialAlreadyExists</span><span class="fn-flutter">PrfProviderError.CredentialAlreadyExists</span><span class="fn-go">PrfProviderErrorCredentialAlreadyExists</span><span class="fn-csharp">PrfProviderError.CredentialAlreadyExists</span></code> | Register hit a credential in <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">exclude_credentials</span><span class="fn-python">exclude_credentials</span><span class="fn-swift">excludeCredentials</span><span class="fn-kotlin">excludeCredentials</span><span class="fn-javascript">excludeCredentials</span><span class="fn-react-native">excludeCredentials</span><span class="fn-flutter">excludeCredentials</span><span class="fn-go">ExcludeCredentials</span><span class="fn-csharp">ExcludeCredentials</span></code> | Flip to <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PasskeyClient.sign_in</span><span class="fn-python">PasskeyClient.sign_in</span><span class="fn-swift">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-javascript">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-react-native">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-flutter">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyClient.SignIn</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyClient.SignIn</span></code>; the OS picker surfaces the existing credential. |
| <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PrfProviderError::UserTimedOut</span><span class="fn-python">PrfProviderError.USER_TIMED_OUT</span><span class="fn-swift">PrfProviderError.userTimedOut</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PrfProviderError.UserTimedOut</span><span class="fn-javascript">PrfProviderError.UserTimedOut</span><span class="fn-react-native">PrfProviderError.UserTimedOut</span><span class="fn-flutter">PrfProviderError.UserTimedOut</span><span class="fn-go">PrfProviderErrorUserTimedOut</span><span class="fn-csharp">PrfProviderError.UserTimedOut</span></code> | OS biometric inactivity timeout, distinct from a cancel | Sticky retry with timeout-specific copy. **Do not** auto-retry. |
| <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PrfProviderError::PrfNotSupported</span><span class="fn-python">PrfProviderError.PRF_NOT_SUPPORTED</span><span class="fn-swift">PrfProviderError.prfNotSupported</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PrfProviderError.PrfNotSupported</span><span class="fn-javascript">PrfProviderError.PrfNotSupported</span><span class="fn-react-native">PrfProviderError.PrfNotSupported</span><span class="fn-flutter">PrfProviderError.PrfNotSupported</span><span class="fn-go">PrfProviderErrorPrfNotSupported</span><span class="fn-csharp">PrfProviderError.PrfNotSupported</span></code> | Authenticator lacks the PRF extension | Fall back to mnemonic onboarding. |
| <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PrfProviderError::Configuration</span><span class="fn-python">PrfProviderError.CONFIGURATION</span><span class="fn-swift">PrfProviderError.configuration</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PrfProviderError.Configuration</span><span class="fn-javascript">PrfProviderError.Configuration</span><span class="fn-react-native">PrfProviderError.Configuration</span><span class="fn-flutter">PrfProviderError.Configuration</span><span class="fn-go">PrfProviderErrorConfiguration</span><span class="fn-csharp">PrfProviderError.Configuration</span></code> | Entitlement missing, AASA stale, or assetlinks malformed | Developer-facing error; surface the <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PasskeyAvailability::NotAssociated</span><span class="fn-python">PasskeyAvailability.NOT_ASSOCIATED</span><span class="fn-swift">PasskeyAvailability.notAssociated</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PasskeyAvailability.NotAssociated</span><span class="fn-javascript">PasskeyAvailability.NotAssociated</span><span class="fn-react-native">PasskeyAvailability.NotAssociated</span><span class="fn-flutter">PasskeyAvailability.NotAssociated</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyAvailabilityNotAssociated</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyAvailability.NotAssociated</span></code> reason. |
| <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PrfProviderError::Generic</span><span class="fn-python">PrfProviderError.GENERIC</span><span class="fn-swift">PrfProviderError.generic</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PrfProviderError.Generic</span><span class="fn-javascript">PrfProviderError.Generic</span><span class="fn-react-native">PrfProviderError.Generic</span><span class="fn-flutter">PrfProviderError.Generic</span><span class="fn-go">PrfProviderErrorGeneric</span><span class="fn-csharp">PrfProviderError.Generic</span></code> | Network or generic failure | Generic "try again later" UI. |

Web exposes typed exception classes (`PasskeyAlreadyExistsError`, `PasskeyTimedOutError`, `PasskeyCredentialNotFoundError`) for `instanceof` matching. Rust callers can branch on the collapsed `error.kind()` instead of every variant.

Two recovery paths are common enough to show in full.

Flip to sign-in when register hits an existing credential:

<custom-tabs category="lang">
<div slot="title">Rust</div>
<section>

```rust,ignore
match passkey
    .register(RegisterRequest {
        label: Some("personal".to_string()),
        exclude_credentials: Some(vec![
            // app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
        ]),
    })
    .await
{
    Ok(response) => Ok(response.wallet),
    Err(e) if e.kind() == ErrorKind::AlreadyExists => {
        // A matching credential already exists; sign in to it instead.
        let response = passkey
            .sign_in(SignInRequest {
                label: Some("personal".to_string()),
                ..Default::default()
            })
            .await?;
        Ok(response.wallet)
    }
    Err(e) => Err(e.into()),
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Swift</div>
<section>

```swift,ignore
do {
    let response = try await passkey.register(
        request: RegisterRequest(
            label: "personal",
            excludeCredentials: [
                // app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
            ]
        )
    )
    return response.wallet
} catch PrfProviderError.CredentialAlreadyExists {
    // A matching credential already exists; sign in instead.
    let response = try await passkey.signIn(
        request: SignInRequest(label: "personal")
    )
    return response.wallet
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Kotlin</div>
<section>

```kotlin,ignore
return try {
    val response = passkey.register(
        RegisterRequest(
            label = "personal",
            // app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
            excludeCredentials = emptyList(),
        )
    )
    response.wallet
} catch (e: PrfProviderException.CredentialAlreadyExists) {
    // A matching credential already exists; sign in to it instead.
    val response = passkey.signIn(SignInRequest(label = "personal"))
    response.wallet
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">C#</div>
<section>

```csharp,ignore
try
{
    var response = await passkey.Register(new RegisterRequest(
        label: "personal",
        excludeCredentials: new byte[][]
        {
            // app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
        }
    ));
    return response.wallet;
}
catch (PrfProviderException.CredentialAlreadyExists)
{
    var response = await passkey.SignIn(new SignInRequest(label: "personal"));
    return response.wallet;
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Javascript</div>
<section>

```typescript,ignore
try {
  const response = await passkey.register({
    label: 'personal',
    excludeCredentials: [
      // app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
    ]
  })
  return response.wallet
} catch (error) {
  if (error instanceof PasskeyAlreadyExistsError) {
    // A matching credential already exists; sign in to it instead.
    const response = await passkey.signIn({ label: 'personal' })
    return response.wallet
  }
  throw error
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">React Native</div>
<section>

```typescript,ignore
try {
  const response = await passkey.register({
    label: 'personal',
    excludeCredentials: [
      // app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
    ]
  })
  return response.wallet
} catch (error) {
  if (error instanceof PasskeyPrfException && error.code === 'credentialAlreadyExists') {
    // A matching credential already exists; sign in to it instead.
    const response = await passkey.signIn({
      label: 'personal',
      allowCredentials: undefined,
      preferImmediatelyAvailableCredentials: undefined
    })
    return response.wallet
  }
  throw error
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Flutter</div>
<section>

```dart,ignore
try {
  final response = await passkey.register(
    request: RegisterRequest(
      label: 'personal',
      excludeCredentials: [
        // app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
      ],
    ),
  );
  return response.wallet;
} on PasskeyPrfException catch (e) {
  if (e.code != 'credentialAlreadyExists') rethrow;
  // A matching credential already exists; sign in instead.
  final response = await passkey.signIn(
    request: SignInRequest(label: 'personal'),
  );
  return response.wallet;
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Python</div>
<section>

```python,ignore
try:
    await passkey.register(
        RegisterRequest(
            label="personal",
            exclude_credentials=[
                # app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
            ],
        )
    )
except PrfProviderError.CredentialAlreadyExists:
    # A matching credential already exists; sign in to it instead.
    response = await passkey.sign_in(SignInRequest(label="personal"))
    return response.wallet
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Go</div>
<section>

```go,ignore
label := "personal"
registerResponse, err := passkey.Register(breez_sdk_spark.RegisterRequest{
	Label:              &label,
	ExcludeCredentials: &[][]byte{
		// app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
	},
})
if err == nil {
	return &registerResponse.Wallet, nil
}

if !errors.Is(err, breez_sdk_spark.ErrPrfProviderErrorCredentialAlreadyExists) {
	return nil, err
}

// A matching credential already exists; sign in to it instead.
signInResponse, err := passkey.SignIn(breez_sdk_spark.SignInRequest{Label: &label})
if err != nil {
	return nil, err
}
return &signInResponse.Wallet, nil
```

</section>

</custom-tabs>


Show a sticky retry when the biometric timeout fires:

<custom-tabs category="lang">
<div slot="title">Rust</div>
<section>

```rust,ignore
// Biometric inactivity timeout, distinct from a user cancel.
match passkey
    .sign_in(SignInRequest {
        label: Some("personal".to_string()),
        ..Default::default()
    })
    .await
{
    Ok(response) => Ok(response),
    Err(e) if e.kind() == ErrorKind::Timeout => {
        // Show a retry UI. Do NOT auto-retry without user input.
        println!("Sign-in timed out: show \"Try Again\" UI.");
        Err(e.into())
    }
    Err(e) => Err(e.into()),
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Swift</div>
<section>

```swift,ignore
do {
    return try await passkey.signIn(
        request: SignInRequest(label: "personal")
    )
} catch PrfProviderError.UserTimedOut {
    // Show a retry UI. Do NOT auto-retry without user input.
    print("Sign-in timed out: show \"Try Again\" UI.")
    throw PrfProviderError.UserTimedOut
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Kotlin</div>
<section>

```kotlin,ignore
return try {
    passkey.signIn(SignInRequest(label = "personal"))
} catch (e: PrfProviderException.UserTimedOut) {
    // Show a retry UI. Do NOT auto-retry without user input.
    // Log.v("Breez", "Sign-in timed out: show \"Try Again\" UI.")
    throw e
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">C#</div>
<section>

```csharp,ignore
try
{
    return await passkey.SignIn(new SignInRequest(label: "personal"));
}
catch (PrfProviderException.UserTimedOut)
{
    Console.WriteLine("Sign-in timed out: show \"Try Again\" UI.");
    throw;
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Javascript</div>
<section>

```typescript,ignore
// Biometric inactivity timeout, distinct from a user cancel.
try {
  const response = await passkey.signIn({ label: 'personal' })
  return response
} catch (error) {
  if (error instanceof PasskeyTimedOutError) {
    // Show a retry UI. Do NOT auto-retry without user input.
    console.log('Sign-in timed out: show "Try Again" UI.')
  }
  throw error
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">React Native</div>
<section>

```typescript,ignore
// Biometric inactivity timeout, distinct from a user cancel.
try {
  const response = await passkey.signIn({
    label: 'personal',
    allowCredentials: undefined,
    preferImmediatelyAvailableCredentials: undefined
  })
  return response
} catch (error) {
  if (error instanceof PasskeyPrfException && error.code === 'userTimedOut') {
    // Show a retry UI. Do NOT auto-retry without user input.
    console.log('Sign-in timed out: show "Try Again" UI.')
  }
  throw error
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Flutter</div>
<section>

```dart,ignore
// Timeout is distinct from a cancel: surface a re-prompt UI.
try {
  return await passkey.signIn(
    request: SignInRequest(label: 'personal'),
  );
} on PasskeyPrfException catch (e) {
  if (e.code == 'userTimedOut') {
    // Show a retry UI. Do NOT auto-retry without user input.
    print("Sign-in timed out: show \"Try Again\" UI.");
  }
  rethrow;
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Python</div>
<section>

```python,ignore
# Biometric inactivity timeout, distinct from a user cancel.
try:
    return await passkey.sign_in(SignInRequest(label="personal"))
except PrfProviderError.UserTimedOut:
    # Show a retry UI. Do NOT auto-retry without user input.
    print("Sign-in timed out: show \"Try Again\" UI.")
    raise
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Go</div>
<section>

```go,ignore
// Biometric inactivity timeout, distinct from a user cancel.
label := "personal"
response, err := passkey.SignIn(breez_sdk_spark.SignInRequest{Label: &label})
if err != nil {
	if errors.Is(err, breez_sdk_spark.ErrPrfProviderErrorUserTimedOut) {
		// Show a retry UI. Do NOT auto-retry without user input.
		log.Print("Sign-in timed out: show \"Try Again\" UI.")
	}
	return nil, err
}
return &response, nil
```

</section>

</custom-tabs>


See the [UX guide](./uxguide_login.md) for the recommended recovery UX.

## Supported specs

- [Seedless Restore](https://github.com/breez/seedless-restore): passkey-based wallet derivation and discovery
- [Nostr](https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nostr): relay-based event protocol for label storage
- [NIP-42](https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/blob/master/42.md): authentication of clients to relays
- [NIP-65](https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/blob/master/65.md): relay list metadata

# Credential metadata

Every passkey flow returns the credential it created or signed in with. Its IDs and attestation hints let you pin a returning user to the same wallet, prevent duplicate registrations, correlate with your backend, and show which authenticator holds the passkey.

<code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">register</span><span class="fn-python">register</span><span class="fn-swift">register</span><span class="fn-kotlin">register</span><span class="fn-javascript">register</span><span class="fn-react-native">register</span><span class="fn-flutter">register</span><span class="fn-go">Register</span><span class="fn-csharp">Register</span></code>, <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">sign_in</span><span class="fn-python">sign_in</span><span class="fn-swift">signIn</span><span class="fn-kotlin">signIn</span><span class="fn-javascript">signIn</span><span class="fn-react-native">signIn</span><span class="fn-flutter">signIn</span><span class="fn-go">SignIn</span><span class="fn-csharp">SignIn</span></code>, and <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">connect_with_passkey</span><span class="fn-python">connect_with_passkey</span><span class="fn-swift">connectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-kotlin">connectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-javascript">connectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-react-native">connectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-flutter">connectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-go">ConnectWithPasskey</span><span class="fn-csharp">ConnectWithPasskey</span></code> each return a <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">credential</span><span class="fn-python">credential</span><span class="fn-swift">credential</span><span class="fn-kotlin">credential</span><span class="fn-javascript">credential</span><span class="fn-react-native">credential</span><span class="fn-flutter">credential</span><span class="fn-go">Credential</span><span class="fn-csharp">Credential</span></code> field. The built-in platform providers always populate it; only PRF backends that don't surface one (CLI / file-backed / hardware) leave it unset.

## Fields

<code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PasskeyCredential</span><span class="fn-python">PasskeyCredential</span><span class="fn-swift">PasskeyCredential</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PasskeyCredential</span><span class="fn-javascript">PasskeyCredential</span><span class="fn-react-native">PasskeyCredential</span><span class="fn-flutter">PasskeyCredential</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyCredential</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyCredential</span></code> carries:

| Field | Available | Use it for |
|---|---|---|
| <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">credential_id</span><span class="fn-python">credential_id</span><span class="fn-swift">credentialId</span><span class="fn-kotlin">credentialId</span><span class="fn-javascript">credentialId</span><span class="fn-react-native">credentialId</span><span class="fn-flutter">credentialId</span><span class="fn-go">CredentialId</span><span class="fn-csharp">CredentialId</span></code> | always | [Pinning a returning user](#pin-a-returning-user-to-the-same-wallet) and [preventing duplicate registrations](#prevent-duplicate-registrations). |
| <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">user_id</span><span class="fn-python">user_id</span><span class="fn-swift">userId</span><span class="fn-kotlin">userId</span><span class="fn-javascript">userId</span><span class="fn-react-native">userId</span><span class="fn-flutter">userId</span><span class="fn-go">UserId</span><span class="fn-csharp">UserId</span></code> | registration | [Correlating with your backend](#correlate-the-credential-with-your-backend). |
| <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">aaguid</span><span class="fn-python">aaguid</span><span class="fn-swift">aaguid</span><span class="fn-kotlin">aaguid</span><span class="fn-javascript">aaguid</span><span class="fn-react-native">aaguid</span><span class="fn-flutter">aaguid</span><span class="fn-go">Aaguid</span><span class="fn-csharp">Aaguid</span></code> | registration | [Showing the authenticator](#show-the-authenticator-and-sync-status). Unverified. |
| <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">backup_eligible</span><span class="fn-python">backup_eligible</span><span class="fn-swift">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-kotlin">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-javascript">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-react-native">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-flutter">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-go">BackupEligible</span><span class="fn-csharp">BackupEligible</span></code> | registration | [Showing the sync status](#show-the-authenticator-and-sync-status). |

## Using the fields

Each of these is optional. The basic register and sign-in flows need none of them: reach for one only when your app wants that behavior.

### Pin a returning user to the same wallet

Each credential derives its own wallet seed, so a returning user must sign in with the same credential to re-open the same wallet.

Persist <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">credential_id</span><span class="fn-python">credential_id</span><span class="fn-swift">credentialId</span><span class="fn-kotlin">credentialId</span><span class="fn-javascript">credentialId</span><span class="fn-react-native">credentialId</span><span class="fn-flutter">credentialId</span><span class="fn-go">CredentialId</span><span class="fn-csharp">CredentialId</span></code> after registration and pass it as <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">allow_credentials</span><span class="fn-python">allow_credentials</span><span class="fn-swift">allowCredentials</span><span class="fn-kotlin">allowCredentials</span><span class="fn-javascript">allowCredentials</span><span class="fn-react-native">allowCredentials</span><span class="fn-flutter">allowCredentials</span><span class="fn-go">AllowCredentials</span><span class="fn-csharp">AllowCredentials</span></code> on <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">sign_in</span><span class="fn-python">sign_in</span><span class="fn-swift">signIn</span><span class="fn-kotlin">signIn</span><span class="fn-javascript">signIn</span><span class="fn-react-native">signIn</span><span class="fn-flutter">signIn</span><span class="fn-go">SignIn</span><span class="fn-csharp">SignIn</span></code>. The OS then offers only that credential. Omit <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">allow_credentials</span><span class="fn-python">allow_credentials</span><span class="fn-swift">allowCredentials</span><span class="fn-kotlin">allowCredentials</span><span class="fn-javascript">allowCredentials</span><span class="fn-react-native">allowCredentials</span><span class="fn-flutter">allowCredentials</span><span class="fn-go">AllowCredentials</span><span class="fn-csharp">AllowCredentials</span></code> and the OS picks any matching credential for your RP.

<custom-tabs category="lang">
<div slot="title">Rust</div>
<section>

```rust,ignore
let response = passkey
    .register(RegisterRequest {
        label: Some("personal".to_string()),
        ..Default::default()
    })
    .await?;

if let Some(credential) = &response.credential {
    // Persist to reopen the same wallet on sign-in
    println!("{:?}", credential.credential_id);
    // Authenticator model (display hint, unverified)
    println!("{:?}", credential.aaguid);
    // Whether the passkey syncs across devices
    println!("{:?}", credential.backup_eligible);
}

// Pin the stored credential ID so the OS can't substitute a sibling,
// which would derive a different wallet.
let sign_in_response = passkey
    .sign_in(SignInRequest {
        label: Some("personal".to_string()),
        allow_credentials: Some(vec![/* stored credential_id bytes */]),
        ..Default::default()
    })
    .await?;
// Pass to connect() to open the wallet
println!("{:?}", sign_in_response.wallet.seed);
// Label this wallet was derived from
println!("{}", sign_in_response.wallet.label);
// This passkey's labels (populated on discovery sign-in)
println!("{:?}", sign_in_response.labels);
// Credential signed in with (credential_id only)
println!("{:?}", sign_in_response.credential);
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Swift</div>
<section>

```swift,ignore
let response = try await passkey.register(
    request: RegisterRequest(label: "personal")
)

if let credential = response.credential {
    // Persist to reopen the same wallet on sign-in
    print(credential.credentialId)
    // Authenticator model (display hint, unverified)
    print(credential.aaguid)
    // Whether the passkey syncs across devices
    print(credential.backupEligible)
}

// Pin the stored credential ID so the OS can't substitute a sibling
// credential, which would derive a different wallet.
let signInResponse = try await passkey.signIn(
    request: SignInRequest(
        label: "personal",
        allowCredentials: [
            // stored credentialId bytes
        ]
    )
)
// Pass to connect() to open the wallet
print(signInResponse.wallet.seed)
// Label this wallet was derived from
print(signInResponse.wallet.label)
// This passkey's labels (populated on discovery sign-in)
print(signInResponse.labels)
// Credential signed in with (credential_id only)
print(signInResponse.credential)
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Kotlin</div>
<section>

```kotlin,ignore
val response = passkey.register(RegisterRequest(label = "personal"))

response.credential?.let { credential ->
    // Log.v("Breez", "${credential.credentialId}") // Persist to reopen the same wallet on sign-in
    // Log.v("Breez", "${credential.aaguid}") // Authenticator model (display hint, unverified)
    // Log.v("Breez", "${credential.backupEligible}") // Whether the passkey syncs across devices
}

// Pin the stored credential ID so the OS can't substitute a sibling
// credential, which would derive a different wallet.
val signInResponse = passkey.signIn(
    SignInRequest(
        label = "personal",
        // stored credentialId bytes
        allowCredentials = emptyList(),
    )
)
// Log.v("Breez", "${signInResponse.wallet.seed}") // Pass to connect() to open the wallet
// Log.v("Breez", "${signInResponse.wallet.label}") // Label this wallet was derived from
// Log.v("Breez", "${signInResponse.labels}")
// This passkey's labels (populated on discovery sign-in)
// Log.v("Breez", "${signInResponse.credential}") // Credential signed in with (credential_id only)
```

```csharp,ignore
    // Authenticator model (display hint, unverified)

    // Whether the passkey syncs across devices

        // stored credentialId bytes
    }
));
```

```python,ignore
response = await passkey.register(RegisterRequest(label="personal"))

if response.credential is not None:
    # Persist to reopen the same wallet on sign-in
    print(response.credential.credential_id)
    # Authenticator model (display hint, unverified)
    print(response.credential.aaguid)
    # Whether the passkey syncs across devices

# Pin the stored credential ID so the OS can't substitute a sibling
# credential, which would derive a different wallet.

    SignInRequest(
        label="personal",
        allow_credentials=[
            # stored credential_id bytes

)
```

<div slot="title">Go</div>

```go,ignore
label := "personal"
response, err := passkey.Register(breez_sdk_spark.RegisterRequest{Label: &label})
if err != nil {
	return err
}

if response.Credential != nil {
	// Persist to reopen the same wallet on sign-in
	log.Println(response.Credential.CredentialId)
	// Authenticator model (display hint, unverified)
	log.Println(response.Credential.Aaguid)
	// Whether the passkey syncs across devices
	log.Println(response.Credential.BackupEligible)
}

// Pin the stored credential ID so the OS can't substitute a sibling
// credential, which would derive a different wallet.
signInResponse, err := passkey.SignIn(breez_sdk_spark.SignInRequest{
	Label:            &label,
	AllowCredentials: &[][]byte{
		// stored CredentialId bytes
	},
})
if err != nil {
	return err
}
// Pass to connect() to open the wallet
log.Println(signInResponse.Wallet.Seed)
// Label this wallet was derived from
log.Println(signInResponse.Wallet.Label)
// This passkey's labels (populated on discovery sign-in)
log.Println(signInResponse.Labels)
// Credential signed in with (credential_id only)
log.Println(signInResponse.Credential)
```

</section>

### Prevent duplicate registrations

Pass the user's already-registered credential IDs as <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">exclude_credentials</span><span class="fn-python">exclude_credentials</span><span class="fn-swift">excludeCredentials</span><span class="fn-kotlin">excludeCredentials</span><span class="fn-javascript">excludeCredentials</span><span class="fn-react-native">excludeCredentials</span><span class="fn-flutter">excludeCredentials</span><span class="fn-go">ExcludeCredentials</span><span class="fn-csharp">ExcludeCredentials</span></code> on <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">register</span><span class="fn-python">register</span><span class="fn-swift">register</span><span class="fn-kotlin">register</span><span class="fn-javascript">register</span><span class="fn-react-native">register</span><span class="fn-flutter">register</span><span class="fn-go">Register</span><span class="fn-csharp">Register</span></code>. When one is already on the device, the OS refuses to create a second and raises <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PrfProviderError::CredentialAlreadyExists</span><span class="fn-python">PrfProviderError.CREDENTIAL_ALREADY_EXISTS</span><span class="fn-swift">PrfProviderError.credentialAlreadyExists</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PrfProviderError.CredentialAlreadyExists</span><span class="fn-javascript">PrfProviderError.CredentialAlreadyExists</span><span class="fn-react-native">PrfProviderError.CredentialAlreadyExists</span><span class="fn-flutter">PrfProviderError.CredentialAlreadyExists</span><span class="fn-go">PrfProviderErrorCredentialAlreadyExists</span><span class="fn-csharp">PrfProviderError.CredentialAlreadyExists</span></code>: route that to <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">sign_in</span><span class="fn-python">sign_in</span><span class="fn-swift">signIn</span><span class="fn-kotlin">signIn</span><span class="fn-javascript">signIn</span><span class="fn-react-native">signIn</span><span class="fn-flutter">signIn</span><span class="fn-go">SignIn</span><span class="fn-csharp">SignIn</span></code> so the picker surfaces the existing credential.

<custom-tabs category="lang">
<div slot="title">Rust</div>
<section>

```rust,ignore
match passkey
    .register(RegisterRequest {
        label: Some("personal".to_string()),
        exclude_credentials: Some(vec![
            // app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
        ]),
    })
    .await
{
    Ok(response) => Ok(response.wallet),
    Err(e) if e.kind() == ErrorKind::AlreadyExists => {
        // A matching credential already exists; sign in to it instead.
        let response = passkey
            .sign_in(SignInRequest {
                label: Some("personal".to_string()),
                ..Default::default()
            })
            .await?;
        Ok(response.wallet)
    }
    Err(e) => Err(e.into()),
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Swift</div>
<section>

```swift,ignore
do {
    let response = try await passkey.register(
        request: RegisterRequest(
            label: "personal",
            excludeCredentials: [
                // app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
            ]
        )
    )
    return response.wallet
} catch PrfProviderError.CredentialAlreadyExists {
    // A matching credential already exists; sign in instead.
    let response = try await passkey.signIn(
        request: SignInRequest(label: "personal")
    )
    return response.wallet
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Kotlin</div>
<section>

```kotlin,ignore
return try {
    val response = passkey.register(
        RegisterRequest(
            label = "personal",
            // app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
            excludeCredentials = emptyList(),
        )
    )
    response.wallet
} catch (e: PrfProviderException.CredentialAlreadyExists) {
    // A matching credential already exists; sign in to it instead.
    val response = passkey.signIn(SignInRequest(label = "personal"))
    response.wallet
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">C#</div>
<section>

```csharp,ignore
try
{
    var response = await passkey.Register(new RegisterRequest(
        label: "personal",
        excludeCredentials: new byte[][]
        {
            // app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
        }
    ));
    return response.wallet;
}
catch (PrfProviderException.CredentialAlreadyExists)
{
    var response = await passkey.SignIn(new SignInRequest(label: "personal"));
    return response.wallet;
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Javascript</div>
<section>

```typescript,ignore
try {
  const response = await passkey.register({
    label: 'personal',
    excludeCredentials: [
      // app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
    ]
  })
  return response.wallet
} catch (error) {
  if (error instanceof PasskeyAlreadyExistsError) {
    // A matching credential already exists; sign in to it instead.
    const response = await passkey.signIn({ label: 'personal' })
    return response.wallet
  }
  throw error
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">React Native</div>
<section>

```typescript,ignore
try {
  const response = await passkey.register({
    label: 'personal',
    excludeCredentials: [
      // app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
    ]
  })
  return response.wallet
} catch (error) {
  if (error instanceof PasskeyPrfException && error.code === 'credentialAlreadyExists') {
    // A matching credential already exists; sign in to it instead.
    const response = await passkey.signIn({
      label: 'personal',
      allowCredentials: undefined,
      preferImmediatelyAvailableCredentials: undefined
    })
    return response.wallet
  }
  throw error
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Flutter</div>
<section>

```dart,ignore
try {
  final response = await passkey.register(
    request: RegisterRequest(
      label: 'personal',
      excludeCredentials: [
        // app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
      ],
    ),
  );
  return response.wallet;
} on PasskeyPrfException catch (e) {
  if (e.code != 'credentialAlreadyExists') rethrow;
  // A matching credential already exists; sign in instead.
  final response = await passkey.signIn(
    request: SignInRequest(label: 'personal'),
  );
  return response.wallet;
}
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Python</div>
<section>

```python,ignore
try:
    await passkey.register(
        RegisterRequest(
            label="personal",
            exclude_credentials=[
                # app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
            ],
        )
    )
except PrfProviderError.CredentialAlreadyExists:
    # A matching credential already exists; sign in to it instead.
    response = await passkey.sign_in(SignInRequest(label="personal"))
    return response.wallet
```

</section>

<div slot="title">Go</div>
<section>

```go,ignore
label := "personal"
registerResponse, err := passkey.Register(breez_sdk_spark.RegisterRequest{
	Label:              &label,
	ExcludeCredentials: &[][]byte{
		// app-persisted credential IDs from prior registrations
	},
})
if err == nil {
	return &registerResponse.Wallet, nil
}

if !errors.Is(err, breez_sdk_spark.ErrPrfProviderErrorCredentialAlreadyExists) {
	return nil, err
}

// A matching credential already exists; sign in to it instead.
signInResponse, err := passkey.SignIn(breez_sdk_spark.SignInRequest{Label: &label})
if err != nil {
	return nil, err
}
return &signInResponse.Wallet, nil
```

</section>

</custom-tabs>


### Correlate the credential with your backend

If your backend ties passkeys to your own user accounts, <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">user_id</span><span class="fn-python">user_id</span><span class="fn-swift">userId</span><span class="fn-kotlin">userId</span><span class="fn-javascript">userId</span><span class="fn-react-native">userId</span><span class="fn-flutter">userId</span><span class="fn-go">UserId</span><span class="fn-csharp">UserId</span></code> is a stable identifier set at registration that links the two. The SDK surfaces it locally and never transmits it. Persist it with your user record, then match it on later sign-ins to tell which user is signing in.

This enables account-level controls the passkey layer can't enforce on its own:

- Cap how many passkeys (and wallets) one account may register.
- Revoke a lost credential server-side.
- List a user's registered devices in their settings.

### Show the authenticator and sync status

<code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">aaguid</span><span class="fn-python">aaguid</span><span class="fn-swift">aaguid</span><span class="fn-kotlin">aaguid</span><span class="fn-javascript">aaguid</span><span class="fn-react-native">aaguid</span><span class="fn-flutter">aaguid</span><span class="fn-go">Aaguid</span><span class="fn-csharp">Aaguid</span></code> identifies the authenticator that created the passkey (Apple Passwords, Google Password Manager, a hardware key). Look it up in the community [AAGUID database](https://github.com/passkeydeveloper/passkey-authenticator-aaguids) for a name and icon. <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">backup_eligible</span><span class="fn-python">backup_eligible</span><span class="fn-swift">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-kotlin">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-javascript">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-react-native">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-flutter">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-go">BackupEligible</span><span class="fn-csharp">BackupEligible</span></code> tells you whether the passkey syncs across the user's devices.

> **Note:** <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">aaguid</span><span class="fn-python">aaguid</span><span class="fn-swift">aaguid</span><span class="fn-kotlin">aaguid</span><span class="fn-javascript">aaguid</span><span class="fn-react-native">aaguid</span><span class="fn-flutter">aaguid</span><span class="fn-go">Aaguid</span><span class="fn-csharp">Aaguid</span></code> and <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">backup_eligible</span><span class="fn-python">backup_eligible</span><span class="fn-swift">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-kotlin">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-javascript">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-react-native">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-flutter">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-go">BackupEligible</span><span class="fn-csharp">BackupEligible</span></code> are unverified and self-reported by the authenticator. Use them as display hints, never as a trust signal.

## Persisting the values

The use cases above require these values to be persisted across app launches. <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">credential_id</span><span class="fn-python">credential_id</span><span class="fn-swift">credentialId</span><span class="fn-kotlin">credentialId</span><span class="fn-javascript">credentialId</span><span class="fn-react-native">credentialId</span><span class="fn-flutter">credentialId</span><span class="fn-go">CredentialId</span><span class="fn-csharp">CredentialId</span></code> is returned on every authentication response, while <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">aaguid</span><span class="fn-python">aaguid</span><span class="fn-swift">aaguid</span><span class="fn-kotlin">aaguid</span><span class="fn-javascript">aaguid</span><span class="fn-react-native">aaguid</span><span class="fn-flutter">aaguid</span><span class="fn-go">Aaguid</span><span class="fn-csharp">Aaguid</span></code>, <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">backup_eligible</span><span class="fn-python">backup_eligible</span><span class="fn-swift">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-kotlin">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-javascript">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-react-native">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-flutter">backupEligible</span><span class="fn-go">BackupEligible</span><span class="fn-csharp">BackupEligible</span></code>, and <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">user_id</span><span class="fn-python">user_id</span><span class="fn-swift">userId</span><span class="fn-kotlin">userId</span><span class="fn-javascript">userId</span><span class="fn-react-native">userId</span><span class="fn-flutter">userId</span><span class="fn-go">UserId</span><span class="fn-csharp">UserId</span></code> are only returned during registration and should be stored at that time.

Use synced storage such as iCloud Keychain (iOS), Block Store (Android), or your own synced backend. Local-only storage is insufficient because it is lost on app reinstall and cannot be accessed from another device.

# Managing labels

Labels distinguish wallets derived from the same passkey identity. <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-python">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-swift">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-javascript">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-react-native">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-flutter">PasskeyClient.register</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyClient.Register</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyClient.Register</span></code> and <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PasskeyClient.sign_in</span><span class="fn-python">PasskeyClient.sign_in</span><span class="fn-swift">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-javascript">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-react-native">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-flutter">PasskeyClient.signIn</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyClient.SignIn</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyClient.SignIn</span></code> manage them implicitly, while <code class="lang-fn"><span class="fn-rust">PasskeyClient.labels</span><span class="fn-python">PasskeyClient.labels</span><span class="fn-swift">PasskeyClient.labels</span><span class="fn-kotlin">PasskeyClient.labels</span><span class="fn-javascript">PasskeyClient.labels</span><span class="fn-react-native">PasskeyClient.labels</span><span class="fn-flutter">PasskeyClient.labels</span><span class="fn-go">PasskeyClient.Labels</span><span class="fn-csharp">PasskeyClient.Labels</span></code> gives you direct access to the underlying list and publish operations. Both calls prompt the user for a passkey ceremony.

## Listing

Fetch the labels registered for the passkey from Nostr.
